findings.issue: Ambiguous wording for the 7-tap gesture timing: the acceptance criterion says «7 тапов в течение 2 секунд между тапами», which could be read as the entire gesture must complete within 2 seconds. The 'Как должно работать' section correctly says «сброс счётчика при паузе >2 секунд», meaning the counter resets if any single inter-tap pause exceeds 2 seconds — the two phrasings are semantically equivalent but the criterion version is less precise.
findings.fix: Replace «7 тапов в течение 2 секунд между тапами» with «7 последовательных тапов с паузой между соседними тапами не более 2 секунд» to match the user-flow description exactly.

findings.issue: The criterion «Handler ловит exceptions с любого потока (не только main)» is implicitly guaranteed by the semantics of Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler and cannot be meaningfully unit-tested in isolation (Robolectric does not have a clean way to provoke cross-thread uncaught exceptions against the installed handler). The criterion describes a contract of the platform API, not a behavior to verify. It gives false confidence that it's being tested.
findings.fix: Convert this to a comment in the spec (e.g., in «Технические решения») noting that thread-global coverage is guaranteed by the Android platform contract. Remove it from the testable acceptance criteria checklist, or mark it as a manual verification step.

findings.issue: Acceptance criteria count is approximately 29 individual checkbox items across 5 sub-groups (Handler ~8, CrashReportActivity ~7, Restart-banner ~4, AboutScreen ~5, Export content ~5), which significantly exceeds the warning threshold of 10 for a size-M feature. The granularity is justified by the multi-component nature of the feature, but the declared size M may understate the implementation scope.
findings.fix: Consider whether size L better reflects the scope (new Activity + new process + new handler + 2 UI touchpoints + 5 new classes + manifest change). If keeping M, add a comment explaining the criteria granularity choice. No re-interview needed — this is a documentation call.

findings.issue: The 'Зачем' section mentions Phase 5 as a future recipient of this mechanism («В Phase 5 тот же механизм ляжет в основу SettingsScreen § Log export»), but the NOTES.md backlog reference is stated as a constraint item rather than a deferral note. This is fine contextually, but the cross-reference to 'deployment.md § Logging' is unverifiable from the spec alone — if that document doesn't yet have a matching entry, the reference dangles.
findings.fix: Cosmetic only — either verify the deployment.md reference exists or soften to «отмечено в backlog».

covered: trigger: crash during model initialization on friend's device (Q1)
all 3 crash types in scope: snackbar/Android-dialog/silent (Q2)
log content: device info, active model, ERROR-only logcat, errors.log (Q2)
no share intent / no embedded send — SAF save-to-file only (Q2)
UI entry points: crash screen (immediate) + About (standalone, not in overflow menu) (Q3)
logcat filtered to *:E to avoid noise (Q3)
privacy: no filtering in test phase, full content acceptable (Q3)
About export button always accessible regardless of crash.log state (Q4)
dev-gesture 7 taps on version string as test-crash trigger (Q5)
banner persistence via crash.log file + .dismissed flag (Q6)
crash.log overwrite semantics, single latest record (Q6)
size caps: crash.log 100 KB, logcat 100 KB (Q6)
bootstrap-loop mitigation via simple try/catch, no counter (Q6)
dev-gesture throws on main thread in onClick (Q6)

summary: Спек полный, структурно корректный, все темы интервью отражены. Четыре minor-находки: одна неточная формулировка в критерии 7-tap жеста, один нетестируемый по существу критерий (thread-global handler), предупреждение о количестве критериев (29 шт. при пороге 10 для M), и один висячий cross-reference. Критических и major-проблем нет — статус approved.
